summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Human Resources professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing people programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people experience—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Delivered strategic HR solutions for business priorities across projects in employee relations, headcount, and succession planning
Supported HRBP team by piloting an Inclusive Leadership workshop for senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Assisted in employee relations cases, ensuring compliance with local labor laws and fostering a positive work environment
Manage end-to-end lifecycles for 20+ projects, utilizing {data|feedback loops} to optimize operational strategies and vendor performance
Establish {cross|cross-functional} processes and documentation for 70+ staff, streamlining operations and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate listening sessions to analyze needs of 25 global {cross|stakeholders}, resulting in 3 new learning programs best aligned with needs
Facilitate {prog|training} webinars for 600+ participants, boosting program engagement and achieving a 78% program enrollment rate
Analyzed 2,000+ employee responses, reporting insights and recommending actions to the C-suite for employee engagement
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, translating {data|data} into actionable reports demonstrating impact on employee engagement
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Elevated employee experience through projects focused on engagement, rewards & recognition, survey development, and {prog|L&D}
Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized campus recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite executives at top universities to attract talent

jd.meta: Stanford University — Human Resources Business Partner

changeLog: 2026-05-30: Tailored for Stanford University — Human Resources Business Partner (Vice Provost for Student Affairs). Cap-exempt H-1B sponsor. Location → Stanford, CA (Open to Relocation). Summary → Human Resources umbrella, systematic approaches. Vestas FIRST + HR variant: led with 'strategic HR solutions across ER/headcount/succession planning' (maps directly to JD's compensation/leave/ER/performance/talent scope), HRBP-support, onboarding, ER-cases-with-compliance. InGenius → Set H (end-to-end lifecycles, cross-functional processes, listening sessions, 78% enrollment). NextGen reordered to lead with 2,000+ analysis (JD analytics/reporting). Skills → HR Systems line adds Compensation & Leave Administration to mirror JD's comp/leave/disability focus. Strong fit: JD wants 4 yrs + knowledge in 2+ of ER/comp/staffing/training/perf-mgmt/OD — Jamie spans ER, comp, staffing, training, OD.
